title: DoorDash opens a limited beta of dd-cli for agent-driven ordering
friendlyTitle: DoorDash tests a tool that lets AI agents help with orders
summary: DoorDash cofounder and CTO Andy Fang announced a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line interface intended for developers and AI agents. The tool can search stores, compare options, build carts and hand off checkout; the beta is waitlist-only for eligible macOS developers in the U.S. and Canada.
friendlySummary: DoorDash is testing dd-cli, a command-line tool for developers and AI agents. It can search shops, build a cart and prepare an order, but it is only a limited beta for now.
whyItMatters: It is a small but clear example of commerce platforms designing interfaces for agents, not just people. The demo’s approval step is the important part: agent convenience should not remove human control over spending and delivery decisions.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows how shopping services may work with AI helpers in the future. People should still approve any order before money is spent.
